description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG only) that draws a ResNet-style convolutional network architecture diagram. Title 'ResNet-34 Architecture' at top in 32px bold. The diagram must show, left to right, 9 stacked blocks: 'Input 224x224x3', 'Conv 7x7, 64', 'MaxPool', 'Res Block x3 (64)', 'Res Block x4 (128)', 'Res Block x6 (256)', 'Res Block x3 (512)', 'AvgPool', 'FC 1000'. Each block is a labelled rectangle. Straight arrows connect consecutive blocks. Add TWO curved skip-connection arcs arching OVER the block row: one from 'Conv 7x7, 64' to 'Res Block x4 (128)', one from 'Res Block x4 (128)' to 'Res Block x3 (512)'. A right-side legend box explains the two arrow styles (solid = forward, dashed = skip).
requirements: All 9 architecture blocks are visible as labelled rectangles within the 1920x1080 viewport with no clipping
The 9 blocks are arranged left-to-right and are evenly spaced horizontally (equal gaps between adjacent blocks)
Every block label text is fully contained inside its rectangle with no overflow past the box edges
The 8 connecting forward arrows are visible and each points from one block to the next
The two curved skip-connection arcs are drawn above the block row and do NOT pass through or overlap any block rectangle
The skip-connection arcs do not overlap the title text
The legend box is fully visible, does not overlap any block or arrow, and labels both arrow styles
No horizontal or vertical scrollbar appears; all content fits inside 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) for a microservices system architecture diagram titled 'E-Commerce Platform Architecture' (32px bold, top). Three horizontal tiers, each a labelled band: top 'Client Layer' with 3 boxes (Web App, Mobile App, Admin Portal); middle 'API Gateway' as one wide box; bottom 'Services Layer' with 5 boxes (Auth, Catalog, Cart, Payment, Shipping). Below the services, a 'Data Layer' row with 3 cylinders (Postgres, Redis, S3). Draw arrows: every client box to the API Gateway; the API Gateway to every service; each service to at least one data store (Auth->Postgres, Catalog->Postgres, Cart->Redis, Payment->Postgres, Shipping->S3).
requirements: All four tier labels (Client Layer, API Gateway, Services Layer, Data Layer) are visible and not overlapping any box
All 3 client boxes, the gateway, all 5 service boxes, and all 3 data stores are visible within the viewport with no clipping
The 5 service boxes are evenly spaced horizontally within the services tier
Every box label is fully contained inside its box with no text overflow
Arrows connect the client boxes to the gateway and the gateway to the services without arrowheads landing on top of label text
Each of the 5 services has an arrow to a data store, and these arrows do not cross through unrelated service boxes
Tiers are vertically separated so that boxes from different tiers do not overlap
No scrollbar appears; the whole diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing an algorithm flowchart titled 'Binary Search' (32px bold, top). Use standard shapes: rounded 'Start' and 'End' terminators, rectangles for processes, and diamonds for decisions. Flow: Start -> 'lo=0, hi=n-1' -> decision 'lo <= hi?'. If NO -> 'return -1' -> End. If YES -> 'mid=(lo+hi)/2' -> decision 'arr[mid]==target?'. If YES -> 'return mid' -> End. If NO -> decision 'arr[mid] < target?'. If YES -> 'lo=mid+1' -> loops back to 'lo <= hi?'. If NO -> 'hi=mid-1' -> loops back to 'lo <= hi?'. Every decision branch must be labelled 'Yes' or 'No' next to the outgoing arrow.
requirements: Start and End terminators use rounded shapes; the 3 decisions use diamond shapes; processes use rectangles
All 11 nodes are visible within the viewport with no clipping
Every node label is fully contained inside its shape with no text overflow
Each decision diamond has exactly two outgoing arrows, each labelled 'Yes' or 'No'
The Yes/No edge labels do not overlap any node or any other edge label
The two loop-back arrows return to the 'lo <= hi?' decision and do not pass through any other node
Arrows do not overlap each other where they can be routed apart, and arrowheads do not land on node text
No scrollbar appears; the whole flowchart fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a Transformer encoder block, titled 'Transformer Encoder Block' (32px bold, top). Stack these sublayers vertically from bottom to top inside an outer 'N x' rounded container: 'Input Embedding + Positional Encoding', 'Multi-Head Attention', 'Add & Norm', 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm', 'Output'. Draw two residual (skip) arrows on the LEFT side: one bypassing Multi-Head Attention into the first 'Add & Norm', one bypassing Feed Forward into the second 'Add & Norm'. On the right, place a small labelled box 'h = 8 heads, d_model = 512'.
requirements: All 6 stacked sublayer boxes are visible inside the outer 'N x' container with no clipping
The outer container fully encloses all 6 sublayers without any sublayer crossing its border
Every sublayer label is fully contained inside its box with no text overflow
Vertical forward arrows connect each sublayer to the one above it
The two residual skip arrows run along the left side and do not pass through any sublayer box
The residual arrows connect to the correct 'Add & Norm' boxes (one bypassing attention, one bypassing feed forward)
The right-side hyperparameter box is visible and does not overlap the outer container or any arrow
No scrollbar appears; the whole diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a data-pipeline DAG titled 'Feature Engineering DAG' (32px bold, top). Nodes (ellipses): 'raw_events', 'user_table', 'sessionize', 'join_user', 'agg_daily', 'agg_weekly', 'feature_store'. Directed edges: raw_events->sessionize, user_table->join_user, sessionize->join_user, join_user->agg_daily, join_user->agg_weekly, agg_daily->feature_store, agg_weekly->feature_store. Lay it out left-to-right in topological order so all arrows point rightward. Color source nodes (raw_events, user_table) green and the sink (feature_store) blue; add a legend.
requirements: All 7 DAG nodes are visible as ellipses within the viewport with no clipping
Nodes are laid out left-to-right so that every directed edge points rightward (no backward-pointing arrows)
Every node label is fully contained inside its ellipse with no text overflow
All 7 directed edges are present and each has a visible arrowhead at its target
Edges do not pass through any node ellipse that is not their endpoint
Source nodes are colored green and the sink node is colored blue as specified
The legend is visible and does not overlap any node or edge
No scrollbar appears; the whole DAG fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a finite state machine titled 'TCP Connection State Machine' (32px bold, top). States (circles): CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RCVD, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT, CLOSE_WAIT, TIME_WAIT. Transitions with edge labels: CLOSED->LISTEN 'passive open', CLOSED->SYN_SENT 'active open', LISTEN->SYN_RCVD 'recv SYN', SYN_SENT->ESTABLISHED 'recv SYN+ACK', SYN_RCVD->ESTABLISHED 'recv ACK', ESTABLISHED->FIN_WAIT 'close', ESTABLISHED->CLOSE_WAIT 'recv FIN', FIN_WAIT->TIME_WAIT 'recv FIN+ACK', TIME_WAIT->CLOSED 'timeout'. Mark CLOSED as the start state with an incoming arrow from nowhere.
requirements: All 8 states are visible as circles within the viewport with no clipping
Every state name is fully contained inside its circle with no text overflow
All 9 labelled transitions are present with visible arrowheads
Each transition edge label is readable and does not overlap any state circle
Transition edge labels do not overlap one another
Edges do not pass through any state circle that is not their endpoint
The CLOSED start state is marked with an incoming arrow originating from no other state
No scrollbar appears; the whole state machine fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a project Gantt chart titled 'ML Project Timeline' (32px bold, top). A horizontal time axis spans 12 months (Jan..Dec) with evenly spaced gridlines and month labels. Seven task rows, each a labelled horizontal bar spanning its months: 'Data Collection' (Jan-Mar), 'Labeling' (Feb-Apr), 'Model v1' (Apr-Jun), 'Eval v1' (Jun-Jul), 'Model v2' (Jul-Sep), 'Eval v2' (Sep-Oct), 'Deployment' (Oct-Dec). Each row label sits on the left; each bar is positioned and sized to match its month span.
requirements: The 12 month labels (Jan..Dec) are evenly spaced along the time axis and all visible
All 7 task rows are visible with their left-side labels fully readable and not clipped
Each task bar starts and ends at the correct month gridlines for its stated span
Task bars do not vertically overlap each other (each row is on its own horizontal track)
Row label text does not overlap the bars or the time axis
The time-axis gridlines are evenly spaced and do not overlap the month labels
All bars stay within the horizontal bounds of the time axis (no bar extends past Dec)
No scrollbar appears; the whole Gantt chart fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing an entity-relationship diagram titled 'Blog Database Schema' (32px bold, top). Four entity boxes, each with a header and an attribute list: USER (id, name, email, created_at), POST (id, user_id, title, body, published_at), COMMENT (id, post_id, user_id, body), TAG (id, name). Relationship lines with crow's-foot-style cardinality labels: USER 1--* POST, POST 1--* COMMENT, USER 1--* COMMENT, POST *--* TAG. Each entity box lists its attributes vertically with the primary key 'id' shown in bold.
requirements: All 4 entity boxes are visible with header and full attribute list, no clipping
Every attribute line is fully contained inside its entity box with no text overflow
The primary key 'id' is visually distinguished (bold) in each entity
All 4 relationship lines are present and connect the correct entity pairs
Each relationship line has a cardinality label (1, *, or 1--*) that is readable and not overlapping an entity box
Relationship lines do not pass through any entity box that is not an endpoint of that relationship
Entity boxes do not overlap each other
No scrollbar appears; the whole ER diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a decision tree titled 'Loan Approval Decision Tree' (32px bold, top). Root: 'Credit Score >= 700?'. Its Yes child: 'Income >= 50k?'; its No child: 'Has Collateral?'. 'Income >= 50k?' Yes->leaf 'APPROVE' (green), No->'Debt Ratio < 0.4?'. 'Debt Ratio < 0.4?' Yes->leaf 'APPROVE' (green), No->leaf 'REVIEW' (yellow). 'Has Collateral?' Yes->leaf 'REVIEW' (yellow), No->leaf 'DENY' (red). Each edge is labelled Yes or No. Internal nodes are rectangles; leaves are rounded colored boxes. Lay out as a top-down tree with siblings horizontally separated.
requirements: All internal-node rectangles and all leaf boxes are visible within the viewport with no clipping
Every node label is fully contained inside its shape with no text overflow
The tree is laid out top-down with the root at the top and leaves at the bottom
Sibling subtrees are horizontally separated so that no two nodes at the same depth overlap
Every edge is labelled Yes or No, and the label does not overlap any node
Edges connect parents to children without passing through any unrelated node
Leaf boxes are colored as specified (APPROVE green, REVIEW yellow, DENY red)
No scrollbar appears; the whole tree fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a UML sequence diagram titled 'OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow' (32px bold, top). Four participants with vertical lifelines (dashed) and a header box each: User, Client, AuthServer, ResourceServer. Time flows downward. Messages (horizontal arrows with labels), in order: User->Client 'click login', Client->AuthServer 'authorize request', AuthServer->User 'login prompt', User->AuthServer 'credentials', AuthServer->Client 'auth code', Client->AuthServer 'exchange code', AuthServer->Client 'access token', Client->ResourceServer 'GET /data + token', ResourceServer->Client 'protected data'. Solid arrows for requests, dashed arrows for responses.
requirements: All 4 participants have a header box at the top and a vertical lifeline extending downward, all visible
The 4 lifelines are evenly spaced horizontally across the diagram
All 9 messages are drawn as horizontal arrows in top-to-bottom chronological order
Every message label is readable and does not overlap a lifeline or another message label
Each message arrow connects the correct two lifelines (correct source and target participant)
Response messages use dashed arrows and request messages use solid arrows as specified
No message arrow or label extends outside the viewport bounds
No scrollbar appears; the whole sequence diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a network topology titled 'Corporate Network Topology' (32px bold, top). Top: a cloud labelled 'Internet'. Below it: 'Firewall' box. Below that: 'Core Router'. The Core Router connects DOWN to two switches: 'Switch A (Floor 1)' and 'Switch B (Floor 2)'. Switch A connects to 3 leaf nodes (PC-1, PC-2, Printer); Switch B connects to 3 leaf nodes (PC-3, PC-4, Server). Use a distinct icon-style shape per device class (cloud, shield for firewall, rounded box for routers/switches, plain box for endpoints). Lines are undirected (no arrowheads).
requirements: All 11 network elements (Internet, Firewall, Core Router, 2 switches, 6 endpoints) are visible with no clipping
Every device label is fully contained within or directly beside its shape with no overflow onto other shapes
The hierarchy is top-down: Internet -> Firewall -> Core Router -> switches -> endpoints
The Core Router connects to exactly the two switches, and each switch connects to its 3 endpoints
Connection lines do not pass through any device shape that is not one of their two endpoints
The 3 endpoints under each switch are evenly spaced and do not overlap each other
Switch A's subtree and Switch B's subtree do not overlap horizontally
No scrollbar appears; the whole topology fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a layered protocol-stack comparison titled 'OSI vs TCP/IP Model' (32px bold, top). Two vertical stacks side by side. Left stack 'OSI' has 7 stacked equal-height layers (top to bottom): Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical. Right stack 'TCP/IP' has 4 stacked layers spanning the equivalent heights: Application (spanning OSI's top 3), Transport, Internet, Link (spanning OSI's bottom 2). Draw dashed horizontal alignment guides between the two stacks showing which OSI layers map to which TCP/IP layer.
requirements: The OSI stack shows all 7 layers and the TCP/IP stack shows all 4 layers, all visible with no clipping
Within each stack the layers are contiguous (no gaps or overlaps between adjacent layers)
The 7 OSI layers are of equal height
Every layer label is fully contained inside its layer band with no text overflow
The two stacks are horizontally separated and do not overlap
The TCP/IP 'Application' layer vertically spans the same height as OSI's top 3 layers, and 'Link' spans OSI's bottom 2
Dashed alignment guides connect corresponding boundaries and do not obscure any layer label
No scrollbar appears; the whole comparison fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a three-set Venn diagram titled 'Data Science Skill Sets' (32px bold, top). Three overlapping translucent circles of equal size, each labelled outside the circle: 'Math & Statistics', 'Programming', 'Domain Expertise'. Place a short label inside each of the 7 regions: the three single-set regions, the three pairwise-overlap regions ('Machine Learning' in Math∩Programming, 'Software' in Programming∩Domain, 'Research' in Math∩Domain), and the center triple-overlap region ('Data Science'). Circles must be translucent so overlaps are visible.
requirements: Three equal-size circles are drawn and each pair overlaps, with a common central region where all three overlap
The three circles use translucent fills so that overlap regions are visibly distinct
Each circle has an external label (Math & Statistics, Programming, Domain Expertise) that does not overlap another circle's label
All 7 regions contain their specified text label
Each region label is positioned inside the correct region and does not spill into an adjacent region
The center label 'Data Science' is inside the triple-overlap region
No circle or label extends outside the viewport bounds
No scrollbar appears; the whole Venn diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a control-systems block diagram titled 'PID Feedback Control Loop' (32px bold, top). Left to right in the forward path: a circle summing junction (with + and - signs at its inputs), then blocks 'PID Controller', 'Plant G(s)', and an output node 'y(t)' on the right. An input 'r(t)' arrow enters the summing junction's + input from the left. A feedback path runs from after 'Plant G(s)' back to the summing junction's - input via a block 'Sensor H(s)', drawn as a lower arc returning leftward. Label the signal between controller and plant 'u(t)' and the error signal 'e(t)'.
requirements: The summing junction (circle), PID Controller, Plant G(s), and Sensor H(s) blocks are all visible with no clipping
The summing junction shows a + sign on the r(t) input and a - sign on the feedback input
Every block label is fully contained inside its block with no text overflow
The forward-path arrows (r(t)->sum->controller->plant->y(t)) are present and point rightward
The feedback path returns from after the plant through Sensor H(s) to the summing junction's - input and does not pass through any forward-path block
The signal labels e(t), u(t), r(t), y(t) are each placed on the correct edge and do not overlap a block or another label
The feedback arc does not overlap the title text or any forward-path block
No scrollbar appears; the whole control diagram fits in 1920x1080

description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + inline SVG) drawing a 2x2 strategy quadrant titled 'Effort vs Impact Prioritization' (32px bold, top). Horizontal axis labelled 'Effort' (Low on left, High on right); vertical axis labelled 'Impact' (Low at bottom, High at top). The two axes cross at the center, forming 4 quadrants, each with a corner title: top-left 'Quick Wins', top-right 'Major Projects', bottom-left 'Fill-Ins', bottom-right 'Thankless Tasks'. Place 8 small labelled dots scattered across the quadrants (2 per quadrant), e.g. 'Cache layer', 'Rewrite auth', 'Fix typos', 'Migrate DB', etc., each dot with a text label beside it.
requirements: Two perpendicular axes cross at the center dividing the area into 4 quadrants
The Effort axis is labelled with Low on the left and High on the right; the Impact axis with Low at bottom and High at top
All 4 quadrant titles are present, each in the correct quadrant, and not overlapping the axes
Exactly 8 dots are plotted, 2 in each quadrant
Each dot has a readable text label beside it that does not overlap another dot's label
No dot or label crosses an axis line into a neighboring quadrant
All dots and labels stay within the viewport bounds
No scrollbar appears; the whole quadrant chart fits in 1920x1080
